body:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="no" ?>
<SnowReport>
	<_LastUpdated>2026-07-21T09:22:51</_LastUpdated>
	<TwentyFourHourSnowfall>0</TwentyFourHourSnowfall>
	<CurrentSnowdepth>40</CurrentSnowdepth>
	<AvSnowdepthinSnowmakingAreas>107</AvSnowdepthinSnowmakingAreas>
	<AvSnowdepthLastMeasuredOn>20/07/2026</AvSnowdepthLastMeasuredOn>
	<DateofLastSnowfall>14/07/2026</DateofLastSnowfall>
	<SevenDaySnowfall>0</SevenDaySnowfall>
	<SeasonToDateSnowfall>96</SeasonToDateSnowfall>
	<Snowmaking>In Progress</Snowmaking>
	<WeatherConditionsasat6AM></WeatherConditionsasat6AM>
	<Temperature>2.7</Temperature>
	<OvernightMin>2.7</OvernightMin>
	<ForecastMax>5</ForecastMax>
	<Wind>Moderate 26-40 km/h</Wind>
	<WindDirection>N</WindDirection>
	<Precipitation>None</Precipitation>
	<ExpectedVisibility>Excellent</ExpectedVisibility>
	<ExpectedCloudCover>Sunny</ExpectedCloudCover>
	<Forecast>Mostly sunny. Winds north to northwesterly 25 to 35 km/h increasing to 30 to 40 km/h in the late evening.</Forecast>
	<ResortReportHeadline>Sun, Snowmaking and Heavenly Valley Opened Yesterday!</ResortReportHeadline>
	<ResortReport><p>It'll be another cracker of a day on the hill!&nbsp; The stars are shining and the snowguns are pumping it out thanks to the very low humidity all night.&nbsp; The current temp is 2.7 degrees, heading for a top of 5 degrees and the wind is a moderate northerly which may increase later in the day.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p><p>I expect the snowmaking will make for good turns on the groomers this morning before some zones get scraped back to the hard base underneath.&nbsp; The sunniest of aspects may soften for spring like turns as the day warms but the shaded and south facing slopes will probably stay very firm and slick for the day.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p><p>Heavenly Valley lift will be spinning once the patrol have completed their assessments, but the area is ONLY suitable for ADVANCED and EXPERT skiers and riders.&nbsp; The cover is thin and the surface will likely remain icy on most runs for the day. The Extreme Area is Closed for ice hazard.&nbsp; And DO NOT ATTEMPT to retrieve dropped equipment from any closed or out of bounds areas.&nbsp; There is a dangerous sliding hazard out there at the moment.</p><p>Slap on the screen, stick to the open areas and have a beaut day in the sun.&nbsp;</p><p>Cheers Bill</p></ResortReport>
	<HideReportEDMWeb>False</HideReportEDMWeb>
	<LiftsOpen></LiftsOpen>
	<LiftsStandby></LiftsStandby>
	<LiftsClosed></LiftsClosed>
	<RunsOpen></RunsOpen>
	<RunsGroomed></RunsGroomed>
	<RunsSnowmaking>5</RunsSnowmaking>
	<TerrainParks></TerrainParks>
	<Backcountry>21 July 2026

There is a significant sliding hazard in the backcountry again today, plus the cover is thin, becoming more marginal the lower you go. The sunniest of aspects may soften for spring-like turns as the day warms, but shaded and south-facing slopes will likely stay very icy and dangerous for the day.  Take Care. </Backcountry>
	<Avalanche></Avalanche>
	<HeliLink>Closed</HeliLink>
</SnowReport>
